sensitivity.method: Monte Carlo, uniform ±30% perturbation on each weight, renormalized

artemis3_comparison.source: NASA Artemis III Candidate Landing Regions, August 2022
artemis3_comparison.caveat: Artemis III sites are optimized for water extraction from permanently-shadowed craters (PSRs), not ilmenite mining. CLPI-ART normal weights favor equatorial mare basalts and thus naturally score south polar sites low. A polar-weighted variant CLPI-ART-POLE (with increased PSR bonus and reduced FeO×TiO2 term) would be a separate index for that mission class.

limitations.desc: Weights (0.35/0.15/0.25/0.15/0.10) are design choices by the author, not derived from formal optimization against ground truth.
limitations.mitigation: Sensitivity analysis (N=500, ±30%) shows top-1 stable in 55% of runs and top-5 persistence >85% for all reference sites.

limitations.desc: Index is not validated against any ISRU ground-truth dataset (no Apollo/Luna/Chang'e sample correlation performed here).
limitations.mitigation: Index self-validates against Jolliff et al. 2000 PKT literature (top-5 all in Procellarum KREEP Terrane as predicted). Future work: correlate with Apollo basalt TiO2 measurements.

limitations.desc: C3 (terrain accessibility) uses LOLA 16 ppd (~1.9 km/px) in 5-px window (~10 km). A real landing site analysis requires LOLA 512 ppd or LROC NAC at ~100 m.
limitations.mitigation: This index is a pre-selection tool, not a final landing-site analysis.

limitations.desc: C5 (latitude factor) and C1 (FeO×TiO2) bias against polar regions. CLPI-ART is NOT appropriate for Artemis III site selection (optimized for near-term oxygen production, not water PSR).
limitations.mitigation: A separate CLPI-ART-POLE variant would be needed for polar missions. The comparison figure documents this explicitly.

limitations.desc: Does not account for optical maturity (OMAT) or regolith gardening effects that affect bulk composition.
limitations.mitigation: GRS measures bulk elemental abundance down to ~20 cm, insensitive to optical maturity. This is a feature, not a bug, for ISRU prediction.

honest_summary: CLPI-ART v1.0 is a proposed prioritization index. It is robust under weight perturbation (top-1 stable in 55% of Monte Carlo runs) and self-validates against the Procellarum KREEP Terrane literature. It is NOT formally validated, NOT peer-reviewed, NOT appropriate for polar site selection, and NOT a substitute for detailed landing-site analysis. It is a defensible first-order prioritization tool suitable for mission-concept screening and resource prospection portfolio reviews.
